rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees referring to component ports, and in implementation scopes for bindings.
Insert AGREE annex blocks in the target component or implementation to preserve AADL architecture.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid AADL scopes based on whether they reference ports or bindings.
Preserve AADL component structure when inserting AGREE annex blocks.
Use the authoritative AADL model to determine component names, ports, and connections for AGREE contract placement.
Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type scopes unless subcomponent ports are properly represented.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Assumptions and guarantees referencing ports must be in component type scopes.
Implementation-level bindings must be in component implementation scopes.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes without proper subcomponent port representation.
Do not reference AADL property names directly as AGREE variables without explicit typed ports or constants.
